edits.slides.0.description: In my vibe coding course, students shipped apps that broke on every device but their own. I designed and built a VS Code extension using Claude Code to fix that — zero context-switch, real device frames, right inside the editor.
edits.slides.1.description: Apps shipped fine on the student's screen — broken everywhere else. Four friction points, one root cause.
edits.slides.7.description: Old workflow: five steps to a number. New: one click to a frame.
edits.slides.7.beforeDescription: Left the editor, opened DevTools, typed a viewport width, guessed at a device — then switched back to code.
edits.slides.7.afterDescription: One click to the panel, pick a device, see a real frame — still inside the editor.
edits.slides.7.afterBullets.1: CSS device frames with notches and stands
edits.slides.8.content: ResponsiveView opens as a VS Code panel. Enter a localhost URL, pick a device, and instantly see the app inside a realistic frame. I kept the surface minimal: device selection, orientation, theme, and refresh — nothing to learn.
edits.slides.12.whatWorked.1: CSS frames made the size feel real. Students took checks more seriously.
edits.slides.12.whatYouCouldntMeasure: Cleaner demos were visible. Long-term habit change is harder to trace.

slides.0.metaItems.label: Tools
slides.0.metaItems.value: TypeScript · CSS · VS Code API · Claude Code

setFields.slides.1.highlight: Remove the switching cost and you remove the adoption problem. The barrier wasn't knowledge — it was friction.

ops.reason: Claude Code build skill must be a visible, discrete beat — not buried in the intro metaItems.

slide.label: Built With AI
slide.title: Designed and built with Claude Code
slide.content: This isn't just a case study about designing a tool — it's about shipping one. I used Claude Code to design and build the entire extension: architecture, TypeScript, VS Code API integration, and the UI layer. Idea to working extension, no separate engineering handoff.
issues: I wrote the prompts, reviewed the output, and owned every design decision
Claude Code handled implementation; I handled judgment
[ADD: screenshot of a Claude Code session or the extension running — shows the build, not just the output]
slide.conclusion: Teaching others to build with AI while building with AI myself — that's the whole point of the course.
